strengths: Strong hook tailored to data scientists interested in AI coding agents.
Clear section breakdown with explicit time allocations that suit the duration target.
Recap and call-to-action effectively reinforce key steps and motivate application by audience.
Good fit for data scientists familiar with VS Code and project-based workflows.
improvements: Research material lacks key findings, so tutorial completeness suffers—ensure source material includes substantive findings next time.
Script could be more explicit about what each agent (@task-researcher, @task-planner, etc.) actually accomplishes—add examples or details.
No code examples in either material; both research and script would benefit from concrete code snippets demonstrating the workflow.
Consider explicitly stating any prerequisites (dataset format, VS Code version, Python environment) so beginners aren't left confused.
Include brief comparisons with manual workflows to clarify the AI agents' value proposition.
summary: The tutorial script is well-structured, with a strong narrative and clear sections that fit the audience and time constraints. However, due to the lack of key findings and code examples in the research material, the script's completeness and accuracy are limited. Adding more detailed agent actions, code snippets, and explicit requirements would boost both effectiveness and completeness.
